title: nervilia abyssinica
intro: Nervilia abyssinica, also known as the African snake orchid or Abyssinian snake orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Nervilia abyssinica include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
intro_study: According to "Veterinary parasitology", Nervilia abyssinica (also known as Rumex abyssinicus Jacq.) is one of the Ethiopian plants that showed significant anthelmintic activity, with a lowest EC-50 value of 1.1 mg/ml, indicating its potential use as an alternative control strategy for cyathostomins in equids.
